format_discipline.detail: reading.md's first non-empty line is '# Functions' — no leading YAML frontmatter block. Learner-facing file is clean.

meta_sidecar.detail: reading.meta.json exists and parses; carries word_count=1880, estimated_read_minutes=10, citations_used=[1,2,3,4], sections=[Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References].

section_template.detail: Exactly the six fixed ## headings present in the required order: Overview (L3), Key Concepts (L7), Worked Example (L146), In Practice (L178), Key Takeaways (L187), References (L195). None added, renamed, or dropped.

length.word_count_recount_estimate: ~1900-1910 (manual re-tally across all sections, whitespace-split incl. code/markdown tokens)

minutes_check.note: reading-critic SKILL.md states the reading-aloud formula as words/230, but .claude/config/artifact-mix.json (the authoritative source, per the precedent set in this topic's own corpus.critic.json which explicitly preferred config over the SKILL.md figure and flagged the doc/config drift as unreconciled) fixes reading_wpm at 200 and derives the deep-profile 9-11 min target from 200 wpm x 9-11 min = 1800-2200 words -- i.e. the word-range and minute-range are calibrated together only under 200 wpm, not 230.
minutes_check.minutes_at_skill_doc_230wpm: 1880/230 = 8.17 min (would fail the 9-11 min floor under the SKILL.md's literal formula)

length.non_blocking_finding: reading-critic SKILL.md's stated 230 wpm formula is inconsistent with .claude/config/artifact-mix.json's authoritative 200 wpm (the same doc-vs-config drift already flagged in corpus.critic.json). Recommend updating SKILL.md to reference the config value directly so future critics don't have to reconcile it ad hoc.

[4] https://realpython.com/python-recurs.note: carries the bulk of the Recursion subsection's citations, consistent with its topic.resources.json notes field

no_forward_references.detail: Checked against sequence.index.json id=3.1 concepts_introduced (function def/call, return values, positional/keyword/default args, *args/**kwargs, local/global scope, recursion, docstrings). Reading uses only these plus explicit backward references to 1.4 and 2.2. No terms from 3.2 (Functional Constructs: lambda/map/filter/comprehensions) or 3.3 (modules/packaging) appear anywhere in the reading.

faithfulness.detail: Reading is a close curated paraphrase of topic_corpus.md; sampled assertions (None-return default, mutable-default-once-at-definition trap, *args/**kwargs star semantics, base/recursive case definitions, factorial(4) call-stack trace and unwind arithmetic matching diagram caption, RecursionError/'maximum recursion depth exceeded' wording, docstring definition, A1-relevance framing) all trace verbatim or near-verbatim to the corpus. No fabricated claims found.

tone_fit.detail: Plainspoken, second-person, concrete-example-driven register consistent with the program's 8th-grade specification voice. Average sentence length is well under 25 words across the document. Two individual sentences run long (Overview's closing A1-relevance sentence, ~46 words; the default-argument-evaluated-once sentence in Parameters, ~28 words) but these are isolated, not systemic, and do not pull the document average over threshold. No hedging phrases ('it is generally considered', 'many experts believe', 'various studies have shown') found.

diagram_embedding.detail: diagram.png/diagram.mmd (recursion call-stack: factorial(4) descend then unwind, matching topic.manifest.json artifact_plan.diagram_intent) is referenced inline at L125-126, directly inside the Recursion subsection, immediately after the prose that explains the call stack mechanism ('The diagram below traces factorial(4)...') and immediately before the RecursionError discussion. Caption arithmetic (1 -> 1x1=1 -> 2x1=2 -> 3x2=6 -> 4x6=24) matches both the diagram.mmd edge labels and the corpus's own unwind trace. Not tacked on at the end of the document.

non_blocking_findings: reading-critic SKILL.md's stated 230 wpm reading-time formula conflicts with the authoritative 200 wpm in .claude/config/artifact-mix.json; recommend syncing the SKILL.md doc (same drift class already flagged in this topic's corpus.critic.json).

Two sentences (Overview's final sentence; the default-argument-evaluated-once sentence in Parameters) run notably longer than the rest of the document's register and could be split for readability, though this does not fail the tone-fit check.
verdict: PASS - all checks pass. Format discipline is clean (no frontmatter, valid meta.json sidecar, exact six-section template in order). Word count (1880 per sidecar, ~1900-1910 on manual recount) sits inside the deep profile's [1800, 2200] target, and reading time is ~9.4-9.5 min at the authoritative 200 wpm config rate (within [9, 11]) -- note the reading-critic SKILL.md's own stated 230 wpm formula would put this just under the floor at ~8.2 min, but that figure is a known, previously-flagged doc/config drift and .claude/config/artifact-mix.json is the source of truth the word/minute ranges were actually calibrated against, so this is a non-blocking finding rather than a fail. All four resources are cited at least once with no orphan markers or bibliography mismatches. No forward references beyond 3.1's own concepts_introduced in sequence.index.json. Faithfulness check found zero unsupported assertions across 10 sampled claims -- the reading closely mirrors topic_corpus.md. Tone is plainspoken with zero hedging phrases; two individual long sentences are noted but do not make the check systemic. The recursion call-stack diagram is embedded inline at the correct point in the Recursion subsection with contextual lead-in and a caption that matches both the corpus's unwind arithmetic and the diagram.mmd edge labels, not tacked on at the end.
